pat-2026-04-21-014.name: The Slides API cannot copy live chart objects between presen
pat-2026-04-21-014.pattern: The Slides API cannot copy live chart objects between presentations. getThumbnail returns whole-slide rasters with chrome/labels included. The correct workflow: (1) get element contentUrl from source slide, (2) calculate target panel dimensions from NB deck layout, (3) insert image sized to fill panel. Also: updatePageElementTransform with scaleX/scaleY distorts panel fill colors - never use it to resize panel backgrounds.

pat-2026-05-07-025.name: Drive API comments.create with anchor field is a known broke
pat-2026-05-07-025.pattern: Drive API comments.create with anchor field is a known broken bug - always shows original content deleted in Docs UI. The correct approach is Playwright browser automation: (1) one-time login via launch(headless=False) + save context.storage_state() to C:/Users/BrianMauck/AppData/Local/Temp/playwright_google_session.json, (2) future sessions load storage_state=SESSION_PATH in new_context(), (3) use Ctrl+F to find text, Escape to close find bar (cursor lands on found text), Ctrl+Alt+M to open comment, paste via clipboard, Ctrl+Enter to submit. Chrome profile at default path cannot be used directly with Playwright - Chrome blocks CDP on default profile. Must kill Chrome processes first with psutil, then copy profile or use saved session state. v20 cookie encryption (Chrome 127+) cannot be bypassed - only option is to have user log in once to a fresh Playwright browser and save session state.

pat-2026-06-11-084.name: When zscaler_reauth.py logs NO_ZSCALER_FOUND and falls back
pat-2026-06-11-084.pattern: When zscaler_reauth.py logs NO_ZSCALER_FOUND and falls back to a stale auth URL, push approvals are useless (they authenticate into a void).
